tagline: Calculate alcohol content from original and final gravity
keywords: homebrew ABV, alcohol by volume, beer ABV calculator, brewing ABV
calcFn: var abv=(og-fg)*131.25; var abw=abv*0.8; var attenuation=((og-fg)/(og-1))*100; return {abv:fmt(abv,2)+'%', abw:fmt(abw,2)+'%', attenuation:fmt(attenuation,1)+'%'};

faq.question: How do I calculate ABV?
faq.answer: ABV = (Original Gravity - Final Gravity) × 131.25. This formula uses the difference in specific gravity readings before and after fermentation.

tagline: Perfect classic cocktail ratios scaled to any serving size
keywords: cocktail ratio, drink recipe calculator, cocktail proportions, bartending calculator
calcFn: var recipes={margarita:{s:2,sec:1,third:1,names:['Tequila','Triple Sec','Lime Juice']},oldfashioned:{s:2,sec:0.5,third:0,names:['Bourbon','Simple Syrup','+ Bitters']},manhattan:{s:2,sec:1,third:0,names:['Rye Whiskey','Sweet Vermouth','+ Bitters']},daiquiri:{s:2,sec:1,third:0.75,names:['White Rum','Lime Juice','Simple Syrup']},gimlet:{s:2,sec:1,third:0,names:['Gin','Lime Cordial','']},negroni:{s:1,sec:1,third:1,names:['Gin','Campari','Sweet Vermouth']}}; var r=recipes[cocktail]; var scale=(ozPerDrink/r.s)*servings; var spirit=r.s*scale; var secondary=r.sec*scale; var third=r.third*scale; var total=spirit+secondary+third; return {spirit:fmt(spirit,1)+' oz '+r.names[0], secondary:fmt(secondary,1)+' oz '+r.names[1], third:third>0?fmt(third,1)+' oz '+r.names[2]:r.names[2], totalOz:fmt(total,1)+' oz'};

faq.question: What is the standard cocktail ratio?
faq.answer: Most classic cocktails follow a base spirit to modifier ratio. The Margarita and Daiquiri are 2:1:1 (spirit:citrus:sweet). The Negroni is a perfect 1:1:1.

formulas: Total Volume = Base Ratio × Scale Factor × Servings

tagline: Calculate perfect coffee-to-water ratios for any brew method
keywords: coffee ratio calculator, coffee to water ratio, pour over ratio, french press ratio
calcFn: var ratios={pourover:16,frenchpress:15,aeropress:12,coldbrew:8,espresso:2,drip:17}; var ratio=ratios[method]; var strengthMod={light:1.1,medium:1,strong:0.9}; ratio=ratio*strengthMod[strength]; var waterMl=cups*177; var coffeeG=waterMl/ratio; var coffeeTbsp=coffeeG/5; var waterOz=waterMl/29.574; return {coffeeG:fmt(coffeeG,1)+' g', coffeeTbsp:fmt(coffeeTbsp,1)+' tbsp', waterMl:fmt(waterMl,0)+' ml', waterOz:fmt(waterOz,1)+' oz'};

faq.question: What is the golden ratio for coffee?
faq.answer: The SCA (Specialty Coffee Association) golden ratio is 1:18 (55g coffee per liter of water). Most specialty cafes use 1:15 to 1:17 depending on brew method and preference.

tagline: Calculate sourdough bread hydration and ingredient ratios
keywords: sourdough hydration calculator, bread bakers percentage, sourdough recipe calculator
calcFn: var water=flourG*(hydration/100); var salt=flourG*(saltPct/100); var starter=flourG*(starterPct/100); var totalDough=flourG+water+salt+starter; var loaves=totalDough/500; return {water:fmt(water,0)+' g', salt:fmt(salt,0)+' g', starter:fmt(starter,0)+' g', totalDough:fmt(totalDough,0)+' g', loaves:fmt(loaves,1)};

faq.question: What hydration should sourdough be?
faq.answer: 70-75% is standard for most sourdough. Higher hydration (80%+) gives more open crumb but is harder to handle. Lower hydration (65%) makes a stiffer, easier-to-shape dough.

formulas: All percentages are relative to flour weight (baker's percentage)

tagline: Calculate International Bitterness Units for homebrew
keywords: IBU calculator, beer bitterness, hop bitterness calculator, homebrew IBU
calcFn: var util=(1.65*Math.pow(0.000125,(gravity-1)))*((1-Math.pow(Math.E,-0.04*boilTime))/4.15); var ibu=(hopOz*alphaAcid*util*7489)/(batchGal*100); var style=ibu>60?'Very Bitter (IPA/DIPA)':ibu>40?'Bitter (Pale Ale/IPA)':ibu>25?'Moderate (Amber/ESB)':ibu>15?'Mild (Wheat/Lager)':'Very Low (Light Lager)'; return {ibu:fmt(ibu,1)+' IBU', utilization:fmt(util*100,1)+'%', style:style};

faq.question: What IBU is good for beer?
faq.answer: It depends on style. Light lagers: 8-15 IBU. Wheat beers: 10-20. Pale ales: 30-50. IPAs: 40-70+. Double IPAs: 60-100+.

formulas: IBU = (Hop oz × Alpha Acid × Utilization × 7489) / (Batch Size × 100)

tagline: Calculate coffee grounds and water for perfect cold brew
keywords: cold brew ratio, cold brew recipe, cold brew calculator, coffee concentrate ratio
calcFn: var ratios={concentrate:5,readytodrink:8,strong:4}; var ratio=ratios[type]; var coffeeOz=waterOz/ratio; var coffeeG=coffeeOz*28.35; var coffeeCups=coffeeOz/8; return {coffeeOz:fmt(coffeeOz,1)+' oz', coffeeG:fmt(coffeeG,0)+' g', coffeeCups:fmt(coffeeCups,2)+' cups', steepTime:'12-24 hours (refrigerated)'};

faq.question: What is the best cold brew ratio?
faq.answer: For concentrate (diluted before drinking): 1:5 coffee to water. For ready-to-drink: 1:8. Use coarsely ground coffee and steep 12-24 hours in the fridge.

tagline: Dial in your espresso with extraction yield calculations
keywords: espresso extraction, espresso ratio, coffee extraction yield, espresso dose
calcFn: var ratio=yield/dose; var flowRate=yield/time; var assessment=''; if(ratio<1.5){assessment='Ristretto — very concentrated, intense';}else if(ratio<2.5){assessment='Normal espresso range — balanced';}else{assessment='Lungo — lighter, more volume';} if(time<22){assessment+=' | Running fast — grind finer';}else if(time>35){assessment+=' | Running slow — grind coarser';} return {ratio:'1:'+fmt(ratio,1), flowRate:fmt(flowRate,2)+' g/s', assessment:assessment};

faq.question: What is the ideal espresso ratio?
faq.answer: Most specialty coffee uses a 1:2 ratio (e.g., 18g in, 36g out) in 25-30 seconds. Ristretto is 1:1.5, lungo is 1:3+.

tagline: Calculate the cost per drink for cocktails and mixed drinks
keywords: cocktail cost, drink cost calculator, bar pricing, cost per drink
calcFn: var costPerOz=bottleCost/bottleOz; var spiritCost=costPerOz*ozPerDrink; var costPerDrink=spiritCost+mixerCost; var drinksPerBottle=Math.floor(bottleOz/ozPerDrink); var suggestedPrice=costPerDrink*4; var profitPerDrink=suggestedPrice-costPerDrink; return {costPerDrink:dollar(costPerDrink), drinksPerBottle:drinksPerBottle+' drinks', suggestedPrice:dollar(suggestedPrice), profitPerDrink:dollar(profitPerDrink)};

faq.question: What is the standard bar markup?
faq.answer: Bars typically mark up drinks 4-5x cost. A drink costing $2 to make would sell for $8-10. High-end bars may charge 6-8x markup.

formulas: Cost per Drink = (Bottle Cost / Bottle Oz × Spirit Oz) + Mixer Cost

tagline: Calculate priming sugar for bottle conditioning homebrew
keywords: priming sugar calculator, bottle conditioning, carbonation calculator, homebrew carbonation
calcFn: var residualCO2=3.0378-(0.050062*beerTemp)+(0.00026555*beerTemp*beerTemp); var neededCO2=targetVols-residualCO2; var cornSugarG=neededCO2*batchGal*3.785*4; var multipliers={corn:1,table:0.91,dme:1.47,honey:1.25}; var sugarG=cornSugarG*multipliers[sugarType]; var sugarOz=sugarG/28.35; var sugarCups=sugarOz/7; return {sugarOz:fmt(sugarOz,1)+' oz', sugarG:fmt(sugarG,0)+' g', sugarCups:fmt(sugarCups,2)+' cups'};

faq.question: How much priming sugar for 5 gallons?
faq.answer: For a standard American ale (2.5 volumes CO2), about 4-5 oz of corn sugar for 5 gallons. Use less for English ales (1.8-2.2 vols) and more for Belgian styles (3.0-4.0 vols).

formulas: Sugar (g) = (Target CO2 - Residual CO2) × Batch Size (L) × 4

tagline: Calculate sugar amounts for kombucha first and second fermentation
keywords: kombucha sugar ratio, kombucha recipe, SCOBY sugar calculator, kombucha brewing
calcFn: var gallons=batchOz/128; if(phase==='first'){var sugarCups=gallons*1; var teaBags=Math.round(gallons*8); var starterOz=Math.round(batchOz*0.1); return {sugar:fmt(sugarCups,1)+' cups ('+fmt(sugarCups*200,0)+'g)', tea:teaBags+' tea bags', starterOz:starterOz+' oz starter'};}else{var tsp=batchOz/16; return {sugar:fmt(tsp,0)+' tsp for '+fmt(batchOz/16,0)+' bottles', tea:'Add fruit/juice for flavor', starterOz:'N/A for 2F'};}

faq.question: How much sugar for kombucha?
faq.answer: First fermentation: 1 cup of sugar per gallon of sweet tea. The SCOBY ferments most of the sugar, so the final product contains much less sugar than you add.

formulas: 2nd Fermentation: 1 tsp sugar per 16 oz bottle

tagline: Tap to find the BPM of any song or set your own tempo
keywords: BPM calculator, tap tempo, beats per minute, tempo calculator, song BPM
calcFn: var msPerBeat=60000/bpm; var beatsPerBar=parseInt(timeSignature); var msPerBar=msPerBeat*beatsPerBar; var barsPerMin=60000/msPerBar; var beatsPerSec=bpm/60; return {bpmDisplay:bpm+' BPM', msPerBeat:fmt(msPerBeat,1)+' ms', msPerBar:fmt(msPerBar,1)+' ms', barsPerMin:fmt(barsPerMin,1), beatsPerSec:fmt(beatsPerSec,2)};

faq.question: What BPM is normal?
faq.answer: Walking pace is about 120 BPM. Ballads are typically 60-80 BPM, pop music 100-130, dance/EDM 120-150, and drum & bass 160-180.

tagline: Calculate delay and reverb times synced to your BPM
keywords: delay time calculator, music delay ms, sync delay BPM, reverb time calculator
calcFn: var q=60000/bpm; return {whole:fmt(q*4,1)+' ms', half:fmt(q*2,1)+' ms', quarter:fmt(q,1)+' ms', eighth:fmt(q/2,1)+' ms', sixteenth:fmt(q/4,1)+' ms', dottedEighth:fmt(q*0.75,1)+' ms', tripletQuarter:fmt(q*2/3,1)+' ms'};

faq.question: Why sync delay to BPM?
faq.answer: Synced delays create rhythmic echoes that feel musical rather than chaotic. Dotted eighth note delays (e.g., The Edge from U2) create a galloping rhythm that fills space between beats.

tagline: Transpose chords and notes between musical keys
keywords: key transposition, transpose chords, change key, music transposition calculator
calcFn: var notes=['C','Db','D','Eb','E','F','Gb','G','Ab','A','Bb','B']; var fromIdx=notes.indexOf(fromKey); var toIdx=notes.indexOf(toKey); var semitones=((toIdx-fromIdx)+12)%12; var chords=['I','ii','iii','IV','V','vi','vii']; var majorScale=[0,2,4,5,7,9,11]; var fromChords=majorScale.map(function(s){return notes[(fromIdx+s)%12];}); var toChords=majorScale.map(function(s){return notes[(toIdx+s)%12];}); var mapping=fromChords.map(function(c,i){return c+' -> '+toChords[i];}).join(', '); var capo=(12-semitones)%12; return {semitones:'+'+semitones+' semitones', chordMap:mapping, capo:capo===0?'No capo needed':'Capo fret '+capo+' (play in '+fromKey+' shapes)'};

faq.question: How do I transpose music?
faq.answer: Add the same number of semitones to every note and chord. For example, transposing from C to G means adding 7 semitones: C→G, F→C, Am→Em, G→D.

formulas: New Note = (Original Note + Semitone Shift) mod 12

tagline: Convert frequency (Hz) to musical note and cents deviation
keywords: frequency to note, Hz to note, pitch to note, tuning calculator
calcFn: var noteNames=['C','C#','D','D#','E','F','F#','G','G#','A','A#','B']; var a4=440; var semitones=12*Math.log2(frequency/a4); var nearestSemitone=Math.round(semitones); var cents=Math.round((semitones-nearestSemitone)*100); var noteIdx=((nearestSemitone%12)+12+9)%12; var octave=Math.floor((nearestSemitone+9)/12)+4; var note=noteNames[noteIdx]; var exactFreq=a4*Math.pow(2,nearestSemitone/12); return {note:note+octave, cents:(cents>=0?'+':'')+cents+' cents', octave:'Octave '+octave, exactFreq:fmt(exactFreq,2)+' Hz'};

faq.question: What frequency is A4?
faq.answer: A4 is the standard tuning reference at 440 Hz. Some orchestras tune slightly higher (441-443 Hz) and some historical performances use A=432 Hz.

tagline: Calculate total set length from song durations
keywords: setlist calculator, set length, gig time calculator, how long is my set
calcFn: var musicTime=songs*avgLength; var betweenTotal=((songs-1)*betweenSongs)/60; var totalMin=musicTime+betweenTotal+banter; var hours=Math.floor(totalMin/60); var mins=Math.round(totalMin%60); var setsNeeded=Math.ceil(180/totalMin); return {totalTime:hours+'h '+mins+'m', musicTime:fmt(musicTime,0)+' min', deadTime:fmt(betweenTotal+banter,1)+' min', sets:setsNeeded+' sets (with breaks)'};

faq.question: How long should a set be?
faq.answer: Club sets are typically 45-60 minutes. Bar gigs often run 3-4 hours with 3-4 sets and breaks. Festival slots vary from 30-90 minutes.

formulas: Total Time = (Songs × Avg Length) + ((Songs-1) × Gap) + Banter

tagline: Estimate streaming royalties from Spotify, Apple Music, and YouTube
keywords: music royalty calculator, Spotify royalties, streaming income, music earnings
calcFn: var rates={spotify:0.004,apple:0.008,youtube:0.002,tidal:0.013,amazon:0.004}; var rate=rates[platform]; var monthlyGross=streams*rate; var monthlyNet=monthlyGross*(splitPct/100); var annualNet=monthlyNet*12; var streamsFor1k=Math.ceil(1000/(rate*splitPct/100)); return {monthlyGross:dollar(monthlyGross), monthlyNet:dollar(monthlyNet), annualNet:dollar(annualNet), streamsFor1k:fmt(streamsFor1k,0)+' streams'};

faq.question: How much does Spotify pay per stream?
faq.answer: Spotify pays approximately $0.003-$0.005 per stream, varying by country and listener subscription type. The average is about $0.004. Apple Music pays roughly double at $0.008.

formulas: Revenue = Streams × Pay Rate × Your Split %
